lede: A look at May 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Lewis Miley and David Backes, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2026 Canvas data breach
events.desc: After a ransomware attack by the group ShinyHunters, the education platform Canvas operated by private company Instructure, goes offline, affecting thousands of educational institutions.

events.title: Faisal Shahzad
events.desc: Faisal Shahzad attempts to detonate a car bomb in Times Square, but the bomb fails to go off.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: Protests erupt in response to U.S. and South Vietnamese forces attacking Vietnamese communists in a Cambodian Campaign.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: German radio broadcasts news of Adolf Hitler's death, falsely stating that he has "fallen at his command post in the Reich Chancellery fighting to the last breath against Bolshevism and for Germany". The Soviet flag is raised over the Reich Chancellery, by order of Stalin.

events.title: Empire State Building
events.desc: The Empire State Building is dedicated in New York City.

events.title: Haymarket affair
events.desc: Rallies are held throughout the United States demanding the eight-hour work day, culminating in the Haymarket affair in Chicago, in commemoration of which May 1 is celebrated as International Workers' Day in many countries.

events.title: Chicago Board of Trade Building
events.desc: The original Chicago Board of Trade Building opens for business.

events.title: Acts of Union 1707
events.desc: The Act of Union joining England and Scotland to form the Kingdom of Great Britain takes effect.

events.title: Nea Ekklesia
events.desc: The Nea Ekklesia is inaugurated in Constantinople, setting the model for all later cross-in-square Orthodox churches.

events.title: Diocletian
events.desc: Diocletian and Maximian retire from the office of Roman emperor.

births.name: Tim Hodgkinson
births.line: English saxophonist, clarinet player, and composer

births.name: Evelyn Boyd Granville
births.line: American mathematician, computer scientist, and academic (died 2023)

births.name: Sidónio Pais
births.line: Portuguese soldier and politician, 4th President of Portugal (died 1918)

births.name: Henry Ayers
births.line: English-Australian politician, 8th Premier of South Australia (died 1897)

deaths.text: Ranasinghe Premadasa, Sri Lankan politician, 3rd President of Sri Lanka
T. R. M. Howard, American surgeon and activist
Magda Goebbels, German wife of Joseph Goebbels
François de Troy, French painter and engraver

observances.text: Christian feast day: Augustin Schoeffler, Jean-Louis Bonnard (part of Vietnamese Martyrs)
Christian feast day: Bertha of Val d'Or
